Operations · Manufacturing · NPI

Jay Anandan

Operations & Manufacturing Leader · Aerospace & Automotive

I take complex hardware from concept to production-ready: designing tooling, de-risking new product introduction, and tuning manufacturing lines until cycle time drops and yield climbs. Five-plus years across Boeing and Bajaj Auto, now reading for a Cornell SC Johnson MBA, bridging hands-on engineering with operations and business leadership.

01What I bring to the table

Turning engineering reality
into operational outcomes.

I sit where design intent, the shop floor, and the supply chain meet, translating each into the others' language so programs actually ship.

/ 01

Production Readiness & Line Optimization

Standing up lines and ramps, cutting cycle time and downtime, and defining the KPIs that keep them healthy.

/ 02

Tooling Design & Deployment

Precision jigs & fixtures, dies, and molds, from concept and simulation through pilot validation and rollout.

/ 03

New Product Introduction

End-to-end NPI across multi-site environments: evaluation, prototyping, gated validation, and launch.

/ 04

DFM / DFA, VA-VE & Cost-Out

Manufacturability and value-engineering programs that rationalize part count, BOM, and make-vs-buy.

/ 05

Cross-Functional Program Leadership

Aligning Engineering, Operations, and Supply Chain across geographies to hit ramp schedules and milestones.

/ 06

Operations × Business

Unit economics, financial modeling, and go-to-market, connecting the factory to the P&L.

02Professional experience

Where the work happened.

Selected programs, told the way I think about them: Situation, Task, Action, Result.

The Boeing Company · 787 ProgramBengaluru, India · Oct 2023 – Jun 2025
Assoc. Equipment & Tooling Engineer
Project 01 · Tooling & Cost

Precision Tooling Program for the 787

S787 wing and panel programs needed lower tooling cost and higher line uptime across multiple sites.
TLead tooling design and process definition with measurable, trackable KPIs.
ALed a cross-functional team of 5 across Engineering & Production; authored the concept design for the 787-9/-10 upper-panel wing-skin-to-stringer WBJ drill jig.
30% tooling-cost reduction and improved uptime, recognized with a Boeing Pride award.
Project 02 · NPI

Automated Integration, Multi-Site NPI

SManual integration was slowing assembly across the US and South Carolina lines.
TIntroduce and deploy an automated integration solution, end to end.
AOwned NPI through evaluation, prototyping, pilot validation, and production deployment; turned operator feedback and constraints into engineering inputs.
60% reduction in assembly time and streamlined cross-site execution.
Project 03 · Quality & Ergonomics

Ergonomics-Driven Design & AS9100D

SErgonomic and quality risks were driving rework, downtime, and audit exposure.
TConvert end-user insight into design requirements and lock in quality gates.
ADrove implementation through validation gates; led a data-driven, SWOT-informed roadmap supporting AS9100D recertification.
$250K+ rework avoided · AS9100D recertified with zero nonconformance.
Pride @ Boeing

Concept design for the 787-9/-10 upper-panel wing-skin-to-stringer WBJ drill jig, delivered with minimal guidance.

Pride @ Boeing

Supported the AS9100D recertification audit (2024), cleared with no nonconformance.

Bajaj Auto Ltd. (OEM)Pune, India · Jul 2021 – Oct 2023
Asst. Manager · Tool Engineering
Project 01 · DFM / VA-VE

Manufacturability & Value Engineering

SAssembly carried excess part count and cost at 250K-unit annual volume.
TStreamline manufacturability and rationalize make-vs-buy.
ALed a DFM and VA/VE initiative with R&D and benchmarking teams; managed make-vs-buy trade-offs across 2W, 3W, and 4W platforms.
Part count −60%, assembly +32%, ~$1.25M annual savings.
Project 02 · HPDC

Engine Cylinder-Block Redesign

SThe cylinder-block casting carried high downtime, material use, and labor.
TRedesign the tool and process via high-pressure die casting.
ARe-engineered the block for HPDC; remodeled tooling in Siemens NX with Magna casting simulation and heat-treat constraints.
Cycle downtime −80%, material −12.3t/yr, manpower 3 to 1.
Project 03 · Cash-Out Saving

Non-Moving Asset Die Conversion

SNew Triumph and Husqvarna MY24 cover-clutch tools would each demand fresh capital.
TReduce cash-out flow by reusing idle, non-moving dies.
ARan RCCA and a feasibility study to convert existing RE235 die elements; secured cross-functional approval for repurposing.
$48K tooling value recovered · ~70% of RE235 die elements reused · new Capex avoided.
Battery-cover rejection 50% to 10% via SPC Alloy-wheel mold redesign 4× productivity EV component manufacturability & supplier integration DOE-validated tooling under pilot conditions

What I do when I'm not on the floor.

Senior Cadet, Naval Wing
